recommendations: deduplicate MCP endpoints and normalize names (e.g.
repeated umap/tsne/pca/dotplot/matrixplot/rank_genes_groups)
add strict input/output schemas with Pydantic models for AnnData paths/options and structured error codes
implement filesystem sandboxing plus allowlisted extensions for read/write/read_visium to reduce path traversal and unsafe file access risk
split heavy/optional tools (leiden/louvain/umap/external methods) behind capability checks with clear “missing dependency” remediation messages
add a minimal automated test suite for generated MCP endpoints (smoke + parameter validation + failure cases) since test status is empty
introduce lazy imports for plotting/external modules to reduce startup latency and avoid hard failures when optional deps are absent
provide async/job-style wrappers for long-running computations (neighbors/umap/rank_genes_groups/paga) with progress and cancellation support
enforce non-interactive plotting defaults (Agg backend
save-to-file return metadata) to make plotting endpoints MCP-safe
add endpoint-level timeouts and memory guards for large datasets to prevent server exhaustion
expose a curated “safe core” endpoint profile by default and move advanced/high-risk APIs to an opt-in profile
improve packaging metadata detection in the adapter pipeline (pyproject exists but was reported false) to increase analysis reliability
add version pin compatibility matrix checks for scanpy/anndata/numpy/scipy in startup diagnostics
include reproducibility controls (global random seed endpoint + per-call seed passthrough)
return richer artifacts (file paths
shapes
obs/var summaries
generated keys in adata.uns/obsm) instead of raw Python objects
add benchmark-driven regression checks using existing benchmarks/asv config for core MCP workflows
strengthen logging/telemetry with request IDs and per-endpoint timing to aid debugging and performance tuning
add security hardening for deserialization and untrusted inputs (size limits
MIME/type checks
optional quarantine dir)
improve README_MCP with task-oriented examples (load→pp→tl→pl pipeline) and explicit dependency tiers
add CI workflow for mcp_output generation validation to prevent drift between upstream Scanpy API and exposed MCP endpoints

success_factors: Workflow reached terminal success state and executed all planned nodes (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize)
Resilient fallback path worked: git clone failed but zip-based import succeeded (333 files analyzed)
Import-mode adapter generation completed with runnable MCP scaffold and healthy stdio service
Core Scanpy API surface was detected and mapped into endpoints (I/O, preprocessing, tools, plotting, datasets)
failure_reasons: Primary repository acquisition failed due to network/transport error during git fetch (curl 18 partial transfer, early EOF)
Metadata extraction reliability issue: dependency scanner reported pyproject=false despite pyproject.toml present
Validation depth was weak: original project tests not executed (passed=false with empty details) and MCP tests effectively minimal
Observability gap: performance metrics and per-node timings were not captured (all zeros/unknown)

node_performance.download_time: Download node experienced a hard clone failure but recovered via fallback; indicates robustness but unstable network/git transport. Exact node duration not provided.
node_performance.analysis_time: Static analysis completed on 333-file tree and identified core modules/dependencies; quality acceptable though packaging detection inconsistency reduces trust.
node_performance.generation_time: Generation succeeded, producing 7 files and import-based adapter. Endpoint list is broad but includes duplication and likely overexposure.
node_performance.test_time: Service run/health check passed, but substantive endpoint/functional regression tests were not executed; confidence in correctness is moderate.

resource_usage.memory_efficiency: Unknown from telemetry (reported 0 MB). Cannot confirm efficiency; add runtime measurement.
resource_usage.cpu_efficiency: Unknown from telemetry (reported 0%). CPU profile absent.
resource_usage.disk_usage: Low for generated artifact set (7 files), but actual source processing footprint higher due to full repo fallback import.
